item.title: 🇩🇰 Denmark Chooses European SAMP/T Over US Patriot for Air Defense
item.description: Denmark selected the Franco-Italian SAMP/T NG air defense system over the American Patriot, marking the first major European defection from US defense systems amid Transatlantic tensions. [Anadolu Agency]

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Says Iran Keeping Hormuz Closed to Save Face
item.description: President Trump accused Iran of keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed to save face, hours after announcing an indefinite ceasefire extension while maintaining the naval blockade. [The Hill]

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Jails Soldiers for Vandalizing Jesus Statue in Lebanon
item.description: Two Israeli soldiers received military prison sentences for vandalizing a Jesus statue in Lebanon, highlighting ongoing discipline issues amid the broader regional conflict. [SCMP]

item.title: 🇩🇪🇪🇺 Germany Faces EU Resistance Over AI Deregulation Push
item.description: Berlin is encountering opposition from a group of 10 EU member states in its push to weaken AI regulations, complicating Europes tech competitiveness debate. [Politico]

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Launches Emergency Energy Plan to Counter Iran Crisis
item.description: The European Union moved to unlock emergency funding for member states grappling with energy disruptions from the Iran war and Hormuz closure. [Politico]

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan President Cancels Africa Trip After China Pressures Overflight Denials
item.description: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te cancelled his visit to Eswatini after China pressured three African countries to revoke overflight permissions for his aircraft, in what Taipei called a clear act of diplomatic coercion. [Reuters]

item.title: 📈 Global Stocks Hit Record Highs Despite Ongoing Iran Conflict
item.description: Global stocks have recouped all losses from the Iran war and hit fresh record highs, with the MSCI World Index now trading 2% above pre-war levels as investors refocus on the AI boom and unwind geopolitical risk hedges. [CNBC]

item.title: 🇨🇳🇯🇵 China Intensifies Targeted Pressure on Japan Amid Taiwan Strait Tensions
item.description: China is escalating pressure on Japan beyond diplomatic disputes, with military sources citing real-time "targeting within strike range" against Japanese vessels after a Japanese destroyer transited the Taiwan Strait. [UPI]

item.title: 💰 US Trade Rep Tells Mexican Companies Tariffs Will Stay
item.description: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer has told Mexican auto and steel industries not to expect tariff removals in the USMCA renegotiation, signaling Trump administration tariffs are here to stay for key sectors. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 With Tariffs Stalled, Trump China Policy Drifts
item.description: As Trumps tariff campaign against China has lost momentum, his broader China policy appears adrift, with no clear strategy for addressing the strategic competition beyond trade measures. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Says He Does Not Want to Extend Iran Ceasefire
item.description: President Trump told CNBC he did not want to extend the ceasefire with Iran, stating the US was in a strong negotiating position and would achieve a "great deal." The comments come as a two-week ceasefire was set to expire. [Reuters]

item.title: ⚔️ US to Indefinitely Extend Iran Ceasefire, Unclear If Iran Agrees
item.description: Trump announced an indefinite ceasefire extension while continuing the US Navy blockade of Iranian ports—a move Iran considers an act of war. It remains unclear whether Iran or Israel have agreed to the extension. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇨🇦 Canada Pushes Military Independence in Arctic Amid US Tensions
item.description: Canada's military is working to demonstrate it can defend its Arctic territory alone, as Trump's threats to make Canada the 51st state push Ottawa to rethink its relationship with its southern neighbor. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇪🇺 Europe Must Take Greater Responsibility for Own Security, Dutch Intelligence Warns
item.description: The Dutch military intelligence agency MIVD urged Europe to focus more on its own security, citing pressure on Western alliances and China's support for Russia's war in Ukraine as growing threats. [Reuters]

item.title: 🛢️ Middle East Scrambles for Alternative Oil Export Routes as Hormuz Remains Blocked
item.description: The Iran conflict has disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, exposing the Middle East's limited alternatives for exporting hydrocarbons. Gulf states are racing to find alternative pipeline routes. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan ends decades of arms export restrictions in major defense policy shift
item.description: Japan unveiled its biggest overhaul of defense export rules in decades, scrapping restrictions on overseas arms sales and opening the way for exports of warships, missiles and other weapons as it faces rising threats from China. [Taipei Times]

item.title: 🇸🇬 Singapore faces threefold governance test as Iran war disrupts global trade
item.description: Beyond managing immediate supply chain disruptions from the Iran conflict, Singapore must navigate ongoing geopolitical turmoil and uncertainty while balancing relations with major powers. [Straits Times]

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi Arabia has strong financial buffers to confront Iran war impact, IMF says
item.description: IMF Middle East director Jihad Azour described the situation as a "multidimensional shock" but noted Saudi Arabia maintains robust fiscal reserves to weather the economic fallout from regional conflict. [Asharq Al-Awsat]

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran economy shrinking 6.1% with $270 billion in war losses, IMF projects
item.description: Iran's economy is contracting sharply under the weight of the ongoing conflict, with the IMF projecting a 6.1% contraction this year. Total war damage is estimated at $270 billion in direct and indirect losses since February 28. [The National]

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelenskyy says Iran war strengthens Russia but Moscow faces $100 billion deficit
item.description: Ukrainian President noted that Russia is struggling with a massive budget deficit that cannot be resolved by short-term geopolitical gains from higher oil prices, questioning where China will stand in the evolving crisis. [Firstpost]

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan PM Thanks Trump for Extending Iran Ceasefire
item.description: Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif expressed gratitude to President Trump for extending the US-Iran ceasefire at Pakistan's request, allowing diplomatic efforts to continue as the original deadline approached. [Reuters]

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US Unilaterally Extends Ceasefire With Iran
item.description: President Trump announced an extension of the ceasefire with Iran, though it appeared unilateral and remained unclear whether Iran or Israel would accept the extension as diplomatic efforts continued. [Reuters]

item.title: ⚔️ NATO Intercepts Russian Bombers Over Baltic Sea
item.description: NATO fighter jets scrambled to intercept Russian strategic bombers and fighter jets flying over the Baltic Sea, a show of air power on the alliance's eastern flank amid ongoing tensions with Moscow. [CBS News]

item.title: 🌐 UK Cyber Chief Warns Russia, Iran and China Behind Most Serious Attacks
item.description: The head of the UK's National Cyber Security Centre warned that hostile nations including Russia, Iran and China are responsible for the most serious cyberattacks against Britain. [AP News]

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Mass Shooting Reignites Gun Law Debate
item.description: A deadly shooting in Kyiv has fueled calls to loosen restrictions on handgun ownership for self-defense in Ukraine, a country already scarred by years of war against Russia. [Reuters]
